problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists in src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts with git-backed and non-git file system coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to capture tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: This is the primary git-backed boundary detection mechanism.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive filesystem walk fallback with directory exclusions
evidence.why: This is the non-git boundary detection fallback.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git and walk results, excludes allowed roots, computes sha256 digests
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot logic.

evidence.summary: Git and walk are combined via Set union: git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot])] : walkFiles(projectRoot)
evidence.why: This proves git ls-files and walk are combined, not replaced.

evidence.summary: Allowed roots are excluded: if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue;
evidence.why: This is the artifact root exclusion mechanism.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() excludes: .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: These are the standard directories excluded from boundary snapshots.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps and returns added/modified/deleted changes
evidence.why: This is the mutation detection logic.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations test
evidence.why: This proves git-backed boundary detection works for untracked files.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies untracked-mutation.txt is detected as added: assert.ok(result.boundary.changes.some((change) => change.path === 'untracked-mutation.txt' && change.change === 'added'))
evidence.why: This proves untracked file detection is tested.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs test
evidence.why: This proves gitignored file detection works.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies .env (gitignored) is detected as added
evidence.why: This proves gitignored file detection is tested.

evidence.summary: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts test
evidence.why: This proves non-git boundary detection works and excludes artifacts.

evidence.summary: Assert verifies artifacts are excluded: assert.equal(result.boundary.changes.some((change) => change.path.includes('agent-scratch.txt')), false)
evidence.why: This proves artifact root exclusion is tested.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() builds boundaryBefore snapshot, runs agents, builds boundaryAfter, computes changes, and emits BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: This is the full boundary enforcement workflow.

evidence.summary: boundary result includes project_mutation violated/clean status, allowed_write_roots, and detailed changes list
evidence.why: This is the boundary audit output format.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage() returns evidence_policy cross_check for technical risk/high cost, single_agent for existing projects, none for greenfield
evidence.why: This is the evidence policy routing logic.

evidence.summary: Route selection: technicalRisk || highCost || acceptanceMissingForPrd || unclearApprovalForPrd triggers careful route, otherwise fast
evidence.why: This is the routing trigger logic.

evidence.summary: Current handoff docs exist under docs/memory/
evidence.why: This is the project memory root location.

assumptions: The git ls-files -co flags capture tracked, cached, and other files as documented by git
The dispatch artifact root is always excluded via allowed roots parameter
Full-tool agent stress testing with real Claude -p agents would expose any bypass vectors
risks: A full-tool agent with file write capabilities could bypass boundary if artifact root exclusion fails
Dirty working tree edge cases may not be fully covered by current tests
missing: Stress test evidence using real Claude -p agents with full tools against actual YOLO projects
Verification that dispatch artifact root path is always correctly passed to buildBoundarySnapshot allowedRoots parameter
Evidence of boundary audit behavior in dirty working trees with staged, unstaged, and untracked changes

result.notes: Implementation exists with git-backed (ls-files -co) and non-git (walk) coverage. Tests prove untracked and gitignored detection plus artifact exclusion. Stress testing with real full-tool Claude agents is the primary gap.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is factually grounded: project claims have exact file paths and line numbers from code, tests, and docs. Assumptions are correctly separated into assumptions array. Missing data reflects real verification gaps (stress testing, parameter verification, dirty tree edge cases). Evidence sufficiently supports the claim that YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git filesystem coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z proves git-backed coverage exists in code
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about git-backed file system coverage.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive filesystem walk fallback proves non-git coverage exists in code
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about non-git filesystem coverage.

evidence.summary: Test case 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' with assert verifying untracked-mutation.txt detection
evidence.why: Proves untracked file detection is tested, validating claim coverage.

evidence.summary: Test case 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations' with assert verifying .env detection
evidence.why: Proves gitignored file detection is tested, validating claim coverage.

evidence.summary: Test case 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' with assert excluding agent-scratch.txt
evidence.why: Proves non-git fallback and artifact exclusion are tested.

evidence.summary: Set union code git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles])] proves git and walk are combined
evidence.why: Confirms explorer evidence correctly represents implementation logic.

evidence.summary: Allowed roots exclusion code if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue proves artifact exclusion exists
evidence.why: Confirms explorer evidence correctly represents exclusion mechanism.

evidence.summary: Project docs reference proves documentation exists as claimed
evidence.why: Validates explorer doc source claim.

assumptions: Explorer assumption 'git ls-files -co flags capture tracked, cached, and other files as documented by git' is correctly separated from project facts
Explorer assumption 'dispatch artifact root is always excluded via allowed roots parameter' is correctly identified as assumption since code verification was not performed
Explorer assumption 'full-tool agent stress testing would expose bypass vectors' is correctly identified as assumption since testing has not been performed
risks: If parameter verification is incorrect and artifact root is not always excluded, agents could bypass boundary detection
If dirty working tree edge cases are not covered, production usage could miss mutations in common git states
Stress testing gap means full-tool agent bypass vectors remain theoretical until proven by real -p agent runs
missing: Verification that allowedWriteRoots parameter is always correctly passed to buildBoundarySnapshot in all call sites
Evidence of boundary behavior in dirty working trees with staged, unstaged, and untracked changes simultaneously
Stress test results from real Claude -p agents with full tools running against actual YOLO projects
Verification evidence that .yolo/demand/ and .yolo/stress/ roots are always in the allowed roots list

result.notes: Explorer evidence is well-structured with exact paths and line numbers. All project claims have primary source evidence. Assumptions are appropriately separated. Missing items accurately reflect verification gaps. Primary blocker is stress testing, not evidence quality. Implementation exists and is tested; remaining gaps are real research tasks, not evidence presentation issues.
